
```{r}
library(glmnet)
library(rsample)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
source("helper_functions.R")
```

```{r}
fuels_data <- read_csv("fuels_data.csv", col_types = cols(site = col_factor()))
head(fuels_data)
```

```{r}
## training and testing data split
set.seed(230)
split_data <- initial_split(fuels_data, prop = 0.75, strata = site)
train_data <- training(split_data)
test_data <- testing(split_data)

# create model matrices 
x_train <- model.matrix(fuel_load_Mg_ha ~ ., data = train_data)[,-1]
x_test <- model.matrix(fuel_load_Mg_ha ~ ., data = test_data)[,-1]
  
y_train <- train_data$fuel_load_Mg_ha
y_test <- test_data$fuel_load_Mg_ha

# fit ridge regression with cross-validation 
cv_fit <- cv.glmnet(x = x_train, y = y_train, alpha = 0, type.measure = "mse", standardize = TRUE)
    
# test and train set predictions 
y_hat_train <- as.numeric(predict(cv_fit, newx = x_train, s = "lambda.min"))
y_hat_test <- as.numeric(predict(cv_fit, newx = x_test, s = "lambda.min"))
```

```{r}
# test-set performance
ridge_performance <- pull_fit_stats(y_test, y_hat_test)
ridge_performance$RMSE
ridge_performance$R2
```

```{r}
# diagnostic plots
ridge_diags <- create_diag_plots(y_train, y_hat_train, y_test, y_hat_test)

#png(file = "report_figs/ridge_resid.png", width = 7.5*600, height = 5*600, res = 600)
ridge_diags$residual_plot
#dev.off()

#png(file = "report_figs/ridge_fitted_line.png", width = 7.5*600, height = 5*600, res = 600)
ridge_diags$fitted_line_plot
#dev.off()
```

```{r}
# conformal prediction intervals for test set

# standardize x (and avoid division by 0)
x_means <- colMeans(x_train)
x_sds <- sqrt(colMeans(sweep(x_train, 2, x_means, "-")^2))

zero_sd <- x_sds == 0
x_sds_safe <- x_sds
x_sds_safe[zero_sd] <- 1

x_train_std <- scale(x_train, center = x_means, scale = x_sds)
x_test_std <- scale(x_test, center = x_means, scale = x_sds)

x_train_std[, zero_sd] <- 0
x_test_std[, zero_sd] <- 0

# add intercept
x_aug_train <- cbind(1, x_train_std)
x_aug_test  <- cbind(1, x_test_std)

# store useful values  
n_tr <- nrow(x_train_std)
n_ts <- nrow(x_test_std)
lambda_min <- cv_fit$lambda.min

# conformal setup
grid_y <- seq(min(y_train) - 200, max(y_train) + 200, by = 0.1)
cvr <- ceiling(0.90*(n_tr + 1))

test_pred <- matrix(NA, n_ts, 2)

# construct PI for each test point 
for (i in 1:n_ts) {
  
  # augment training set with one test point
  x_aug <- rbind(x_aug_train, x_aug_test[i, ])
  n_aug <- n_tr + 1
  
  # ridge penalty matrix
  penalty_mat <- lambda_min*diag(ncol(x_aug))
  penalty_mat[1, 1] <- 0
  
  # ridge hat/residual matrix
  gram_inv_aug <- MASS::ginv(t(x_aug) %*% x_aug + penalty_mat)
  hat_mat <- x_aug %*% gram_inv_aug %*% t(x_aug)
  res_mat <- diag(n_aug) - hat_mat
  
  # candidate residual ranks
  grid_r <- sapply(grid_y, function(y) {
    res <- as.numeric(res_mat %*% c(y_train, y))
    rank(abs(res))[n_aug]
  })
  
  # keep candidate y values with acceptable conformal rank
  valid_y <- grid_y[grid_r <= cvr]
  test_pred[i, ] <- if (length(valid_y) == 0) c(NA, NA) else range(valid_y)
  
}
```

```{r}
# plot conformal prediction interval
conformal_plot_df <- data.frame(y = y_test,
                                y_hat = y_hat_test,
                                lower = pmax(test_pred[, 1], 0), # clip to 0 for plotting
                                upper = test_pred[, 2]) %>% 
                      arrange(y_hat)

conformal_int_plot <- ggplot(conformal_plot_df, aes(x = y_hat)) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") + 
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), 
                    fill = 'steelblue', alpha = 0.3) +
    geom_point(aes(y = y), alpha = 0.4) +
    labs(x = "Predicted surface fuel load (Mg/ha)",
         y = "Measured surface fuel load (Mg/ha)") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1.2),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
          legend.position = "top") +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 265), breaks = seq(0, 250, by = 50)) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 265), breaks = seq(0, 250, by = 50))
  
#png(file = "report_figs/ridge_conformal_int.png", width = 7.5*600, height = 5*600, res = 600)
conformal_int_plot
#dev.off()
```
